rulings.Case Title: ANDY SIU VS. OSCAR MARTINEZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternqative Summary Adjudication Of Claims By Plaintiff Andy Siu Against Oscar Martinez
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 23, 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFF ANDY SIU MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication Of Claims By Plaintiff Andy Siu Against Oscar Martinez. The motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant Martinez admits that he was a general partner of M&M Construction and a judgment was entered against that entity. Defendant is jointly and severally liable under Cal. Corp. Code sec. 16306. The court rejects Defendant's argument that the renewed judgment was void. First, it is unclear whether the judgment that was renewed actually has the correct amount of money. The original judgment was for $221,088.46 and the judgment that was renewed was for $151,742.72 plus interest after judgment (total $303,390.55). Even if the renewed amount was improper, then M&M should have objected and sought to vacate or modify the renewal. Second, the Court of Appeal affirmed the judgment and an error regarding interest does not render the entire judgment void. See Dakota Payphone, LLC v. Alcaraz (2011) 192 Cal.App.4th 493, 508. Plaintiff's continued prosecution of this action against M&M after defendant filed for bankruptcy did not violate the bankruptcy stay. See Danko v. O'Reilly (2014) 232 Cal.App.4th 732, 745. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: EDITIONS LIMITED WEST, INC. VS. SOMERSET STUDIOS INCORPORATED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award, Response In Opposition To Petition To Correct
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 23, 2015, Line 2, RESPONDENT SOMERSET STUDIOS INCORPORATED, STEPHEN WEST Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award, Response In Opposition To Petition To Correct. Respondent's Petition to Confirm Arbitration Award is granted, as no opposition was filed, and there are no grounds upon which to correct or vacate the award. The Petition to Correct the award is not properly before the Court, and furthermore the assertions made therein lack merit. First, the Arbitrator's decision to award certain costs to Respondent is not what is encompassed by miscalculation or mistake pursuant to CCP § 1286.6. Additionally, the language in the Final Award ordering certain fees and costs to be "borne as incurred" was related only to Petitioner's claims against Respondent and thus is not a mistake or miscalculation. Second, the Arbitrator did not exceed her powers in awarding costs and fees only to Respondent. The arbitration agreement provided that the prevailing party shall be entitled to recover fees and costs. Thus, the arbitrator's decision not to award fees or costs to Petitioner cannot be corrected by the Court. (See Moore v. First Bank of San Luis Obispo (2000) 22 Cal.4th 782, 782 ["[w]here the entitlement of a party to attorney fees ... is within the scope of the issues submitted for binding arbitration, the arbitrators do not 'exceed ... their powers' ... by denying the party's request for fees, even where such a denial would be reversible legal error if made by a court in civil litigation."].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: ANNETTE HORNSBY VS. JOE'S CRAB SHACK
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Defendants Form Interrogatories, Set One, Special Interrogatories, Set One, And Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, And Motion For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, January 26, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT CRAB ADDISON D/B/A JOE'S CRAB SHACK's Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Responses To Defendant's Form Interrogatories, Set One, Special Interrogatories, Set One, And Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, And Motion For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge David Helbraun, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Plaintiff's Responses to Defendants Form Interrogatories, Set One, Special Interrogatories, Set One, and RPD Set One, and for Monetary Sanctions is unopposed and is granted. Plaintiff is ordered to provide substantive written responses within two weeks of the date of this order, with no objections, objections having been waived by the failure to timely respond. Monetary sanctions against plaintiff are ordered in the requested amount of $879.20 for fees and costs incurred. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to dmh@helbraunlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: REBECCA HARRIS VS. SUSTAINABILITY ACCOUNTING STANDARDS BOARD, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Depositions And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, January 26, 2015, Line 2, PLAINTIFF REBECCA HARRIS' Motion To Compel Depositions And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge David Helbraun, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's Motion to Compel depositions is granted. The witnesses are SASB employees whose testimony appears reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. Defendant may not dictate which of its employees' depositions plaintiff may take, or in what order, and has not sought the Court's intervention in that regard. The cases relied upon by defendant are inapposite, as they involve the burden of overbroad inspection subpoenas to third parties, not deposition discovery from a party. Defendant's position on this motion is without substantial justification. SASB is ordered to produce Douglas Park, Jerome Lavigne-Delville, Amanda Medress, Deb Martin, Eli Reisman, and Harrison Thomas for their depositions on dates within 20 days from the date of this Order, said dates to be mutually agreed upon between the parties' counsel. Sanctions are awarded to plaintiff from defense counsel in the amount of $5,060.00. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to dmh@helbraunlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: OSMAN KARAKUS VS. MG-252 CAL,LLC (DOING BUSINESS AS MICHAEL MINA) et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Osman Karakus' Motion To Compel Further Response To Request, Demand Inspection, Production Of Documents (For Entry Of Tentative Ruling Only)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, January 26, 2015, Line 3, PLAINTIFF OSMAN KARAKUS' MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO REQUEST, DEMAND INSPECTION, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS (For Entry Of Tentative Ruling Only) (Part 2 of 2). The further substantive written responses ordered herein shall be provided within two weeks from the date of this order. The notice was defective in that a motion to compel production, in addition to the motion for further written responses, was not presented. Nor was authority presented in the Points & Authorities regarding compelling production of documents. That being said, if defendants fail to timely produce responsive documents in a reasonable period of time, such failure should be taken into account as an abuse of discovery if it becomes necessary for Plaintiff to pursue a further motion in this regard. Monetary sanctions are granted to Plaintiff from defense counsel in the amount of $3,960.00, because defendants lacked substantial justification for their position in the majority of the issues presented, or could have been resolved by recourse to Plaintiff's offered confidentiality agreement. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to dmh@helbraunlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (End of tentative ruling) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: OSMAN KARAKUS VS. MG-252 CAL,LLC (DOING BUSINESS AS MICHAEL MINA) et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO REQUEST, DEMAND INSPECTION, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, January 26, 2015, Line 3, PLAINTIFF OSMAN KARAKUS' MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO REQUEST, DEMAND INSPECTION, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS (Part 1 of 2). Pro Tem Judge David Helbraun, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is granted in part and denied in part. RPD #s 21-24 to MG-252 LLC: Further written substantive responses in conformance with the Code of Civil Procedure are required as to the existence of any documents constituting or referring to any such performance reviews or disciplinary actions taken (in other words, the requests are hereby modified to remove the term "relating to" as overbroad in this context, and substantive written responses thereto, as hereby modified, are required). The documents, if any, so identified, when produced, shall be treated as confidential for use in this litigation only and to be disclosed to this litigation's parties, attorneys, and consulting or designating experts only, or as the parties may agree in writing. RPD #s 29-32, 34-40, 44-46, 48-55, and 71 to MG-252 LLC and #32 to MINA GROUP LLC: Further written substantive responses in conformance with the Code of Civil Procedure are required as to the existence of any such specified documents referring to Plaintiff (in other words, the requests are hereby modified to remove the term "or relating to" as overbroad in this context, and substantive written responses thereto, as hereby modified, are required). Further written substantive responses in conformance with the Code of Civil Procedure are required as to the existence of any such specified documents referring to Plaintiff (in other words, the requests are hereby modified to remove the term "or relating to" as overbroad in this context, and substantive written responses thereto, as hereby modified, are required). Four Specified Privilege Log Items: The claim of privilege and objections as to the 4/11/12 letter authored by Mr. Polychroniou are overruled as there is no showing that the author or recipient are attorneys. The privilege claims as to the other three items (dated 4/27/12, 6/29/12, and 8/4/12) are affirmed unless those documents refer to Plaintiff, in which case the privilege claim is overruled and the documents, when produced, shall be treated as confidential in the same manner specified above as to RPD #s21-24 to MG-252 LLC. (Continued to Part 2 of 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: OSMAN KARAKUS VS. MG-252 CAL,LLC (DOING BUSINESS AS MICHAEL MINA) et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Osman Karakus' Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Re: Defendant Mg-252 Cal, Llc And The Mina Group, Llc'S Notice Of Further Deposition Of Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, January 26, 2015, Line 3, PLAINTIFF OSMAN KARAKUS' Motion For Protective Order Re: Defendant MG-252 Cal, LLC And The Mina Group, LLC'S Notice Of Further Deposition Of Plaintiff. Pro Tem Judge David Helbraun, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's Motion for a Protective Order, to prevent additional deposition of plaintiff noticed four months after the original deposition session, is granted. A natural person may be deposed only once. CCP 2025.610(a). Plaintiff was deposed on August 26, 2014 and the deposition was halted by defense counsel shortly after 5:00 p.m. that day. Absent an alternative stipulation between the parties, if defendant intended to further depose plaintiff, then the deposition was to have continued on the following business day, as per defendant's deposition notice. Sprague v. Equifax, Inc. (1985) 166 Cal.App.3d 1012, 1041; Dunne on Depositions in California (Thompson Reuters Nov. 2014) §6:25 (relied upon by defendant). Defendant does not contend that any effort was made to do so, nor that plaintiff then refused to appear the following day. In the absence of an alternative stipulation between the parties, it was defendant's burden as the noticing party to timely seek completion of the deposition or to timely move to compel its completion. Defendant's four-month delay before seeking to complete plaintiff's deposition constitutes laches and is prejudicial to plaintiff and effectively defeats the intent of CCP 2025.610(a). Plaintiff's meet and confer was satisfactory. Sanctions are awarded in favor of plaintiff against defense counsel in the amount of $2,000.00 pursuant to CCP 2025.420(h). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to dmh@helbraunlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
